params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Student's Warmup data: Red=40, Blue=30, Green=50, Yellow=20. Axis labeled 0-60 in 10s. Red bar highlighted with axis line at 40 glowing.

steps.text: You saw this bar graph in Warmup. Now let's learn how to read it exactly.
See these numbers along the side? They show the scale. 10, 20, 30, 40. That's counting by 10s!
Red has 40. The height of the bar ends at the 40 line.
I can count by 10s to check: 10, 20, 30, 40. You already know how to count by 10s!

elements.description: Vertical bar graph in reading mode. Red=40, Blue=30, Green=50, Yellow=20. Red bar highlighted with 40 axis line glowing. Scale of 10, axis 0-60.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3: likely counting symbols from picture graph instead of reading bar height

params.description: Blue bar remains highlighted, 30 axis line pulses

steps.text: Let's think about this together. On a bar graph, look where the bar ends. The Blue bar reaches the 30 line.

incorrects.description: Student selected 20: misread the axis line below the bar's actual height

steps.text: Here's a hint: the bar goes higher than 20. Follow the Blue bar to its top. Which line does it reach?

steps.text: You're working on it. The bar's top lines up exactly with a number. It stops at 30, not between lines.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation traces from top of Blue bar horizontally to axis, highlighting 30 line

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Start at the top of the Blue bar. Follow it to the left. It meets the axis at 30. That's the value.

incorrects.description: Student selected 2: counted the number of jumps instead of the value

params.description: Axis lines glow sequentially: 10, 20.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. Each line shows the value we count. The bar reaches the second line. Count: 10, 20.

incorrects.description: Student selected 12: added 10 and 2 instead of counting by 10s

params.description: Axis lines glow sequentially: 10, 20.

steps.text: Here's a hint: we count by 10s on this graph, not by 1s. The bar reaches the second line. Count by 10s: 10, 20.

incorrects.description: Student selected 25: estimated between lines instead of reading the scale

params.description: Yellow bar highlighted, top aligns exactly with second axis line at 20.

steps.text: You're working on it. The bar top touches a line exactly. Count by 10s to that line: 10, 20.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Axis lines glow sequentially: 10, 20. Yellow bar pulses at height 20.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. The Yellow bar reaches the second line. We count by 10s: 10, 20. The bar shows 20.

incorrects.description: Student set Swings bar to wrong height three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets Swings bar to height 30, stopping at the 30 mark on the axis.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Swings has 30 votes. I set the bar to end at 30 on the axis. Count the gridlines: 10, 20, 30. The top of the bar lines up with 30.

incorrects.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to wrong height on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Check the table: how many Nonfiction books?

incorrects.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to wrong height on second attempt

steps.text: Let's think about this together. The table shows 60 Nonfiction books. Find the line marked 60 on the graph.

incorrects.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to wrong height three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets Nonfiction bar to height 60, aligning with the 60 gridline.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. I look at the table: Nonfiction has 60 books. Then I find 60 on the axis. I set the bar to that line. There we go.

incorrects.description: Student selected 20: likely read the Comics value instead of finding the difference

params.description: Nonfiction and Comics bars remain highlighted.

steps.text: You're working on it. We need the difference between the two categories, not just one value. Find how much more Nonfiction has than Comics.

incorrects.description: Student selected 60: likely read the Nonfiction value instead of finding the difference

params.description: Nonfiction and Comics bars remain highlighted.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. That's the Nonfiction total. We need to find how many more Nonfiction books there are than Comics books.

incorrects.description: Student selected 80: likely added the two values instead of finding the difference

params.description: Nonfiction and Comics bars remain highlighted.

steps.text: Here's a hint: we're looking for the difference, not the total. Nonfiction is 60, Comics is 20. How much bigger is 60 than 20?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Visual shows the gap between Nonfiction bar (60) and Comics bar (20), highlighting the 40-unit difference.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Nonfiction has 60 books. Comics has 20 books. To find how many more, we subtract: 60 minus 20 equals 40. The answer is 40.
You can read bars that end right at the lines. But what if a bar ends between the lines?

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Stickers Collected: Emma=30 (at 30 line), Jake=25 (between 20-30 lines), Mia=40 (at 40 line), Leo=35 (between 30-40 lines). Scale of 10, axis 0-50. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible. Jake's bar highlighted.

steps.text: Look at Jake's bar. It doesn't end at a line. It ends between the lines.
Remember half-symbols from scale of 2? Half of 2 was 1. Same idea here. Half of 10 is 5.
Half of 10 is 5. So between 20 and 30, that's 20 plus 5, which equals 25. Jake has 25 stickers.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph in reading mode. Stickers Collected. Emma=30, Jake=25, Mia=40, Leo=35. Jake's bar highlighted, ending between 20 and 30 lines. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. The bar ends halfway between 30 and 40. Half of 10 is 5. So 30 plus 5 equals 35. The answer is 35.

params.description: All rows in data table highlight to draw attention to values.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. Monday is 40, that's at a line. Tuesday is 35, that's between 30 and 40. Use the ghost gridlines to find the right spots.

incorrects.description: Student set at least one bar incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates: Monday bar rises to 40 (at line). Tuesday bar rises to 35 (between 30 and 40). Wednesday bar rises to 50 (at line). Thursday bar rises to 25 (between 20 and 30).

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Monday is 40, right at the line. Tuesday is 35, between the 30 and 40 lines. Wednesday is 50, at the line. Thursday is 25, between 20 and 30. The gridlines help you see both kinds of values.

incorrects.description: Student selected 35: only counted Tuesday, missed Monday

params.description: Both Monday and Tuesday bars pulse to emphasize both are needed.

steps.text: Here's a hint: the question asks for total minutes. You need to add Monday and Tuesday together.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Monday is 40 minutes. Tuesday is 35 minutes. When we add them, 40 plus 35 equals 75 minutes total.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation shows 50 line, 60 line, then marks the exact midpoint at 55 where Park bar ends.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. The bar ends between 50 and 60. When something is exactly halfway between two numbers, we find the middle. 50 plus 10 is 60, so halfway is 50 plus 5, which is 55. The Park bar represents 55.

params.description: System demonstrates setting all four bars: Kai to 35 (between 30 and 40), Ava to 50 (at the line), Noah to 25 (between 20 and 30), Zoe to 45 (between 40 and 50).

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Kai walked 35 miles. I count by 10s to 30, then halfway to 40. That's 35. Ava walked 50, right at the line. Noah walked 25, halfway between 20 and 30. Zoe walked 45, halfway between 40 and 50. Now you see the pattern.

incorrects.description: Student selected 15: likely miscalculated the difference or confused values

params.description: Ava and Noah bars remain highlighted for comparison.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. Find Ava's bar. It reaches 50. Now find Noah's bar. It reaches 25. How much longer is Ava's bar?

incorrects.description: Student selected 50: chose Ava's total instead of the difference

params.description: Ava and Noah bars highlighted to show both values.

steps.text: You're working on it. 50 is how many miles Ava walked total. We need to find how many more miles she walked than Noah. Subtract Noah's miles from Ava's miles.

incorrects.description: Student selected 75: likely added instead of subtracting

params.description: Ava and Noah bars highlighted for comparison.

steps.text: Here's a hint: when we ask how many more, we compare the two amounts by subtracting. Ava walked 50 miles and Noah walked 25 miles. What is 50 minus 25?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Visual shows Ava's bar at 50, Noah's bar at 25, with the difference of 25 highlighted between them.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Ava walked 50 miles. Noah walked 25 miles. To find how many more Ava walked, subtract: 50 minus 25 equals 25. The answer is 25 more miles.

steps.text: Here's a hint: Peppers is 35 and Carrots is 45. Add those two numbers together.

incorrects.description: Student selected 75: calculation error or estimation

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System shows 35 + 45 = 80 with visual annotation on the two bars.

steps.text: Let me walk you through this one. Peppers is 35 plants. Carrots is 45 plants. I add 35 plus 45, which equals 80. The answer is 80 plants in all. See how that works?

params.description: Final graph remains briefly visible.

steps.text: You made bar graphs with scale of 10. You used the axis to find values, even values between the lines. Let's see what you know.
